title: ranunculus aquatilis var. codyanus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. codyanus, also known as Cody's water-crowfoot, is a rare aquatic plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that have been shown to have wound-healing and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis var. codyanus include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that are taken orally or applied topically to the affected area.
